assessment_items.question_content:
**At this time, the Florentine government had just initiated a new tax and this painting may show that Christ rejected that kind of civic responsibility.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/848be18faf33e60c2dfa70ddf2932a3a.jpg)

hints:
**This is incorrect. At this time, the Florentine government had just initiated a new tax and this painting may show that Christ approved of that kind of civic responsibility.**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Masaccio's *Tribute Money*?**



![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/848be18faf33e60c2dfa70ddf2932a3a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**The light and shadows align with the window in the Brancacci Chapel.**


**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/masaccio--the-tribute-money--1427)**

choices.content: This scene shows Christ and the apostles confronted by a tax collector, but Christ has renounced worldly possessions.The money for the tax is found in a fish, which is a miracle.
This scene shows three distinct events associated with this story: Christ ordering Peter to find a fish, Peter finding the money in the fish's mouth, and Peter paying the tax collector.
Masaccio tries to create the most realistic scene that he can using linear perspective, atmospheric perspective, emotions, gestures, and contrapposto.
Although Masaccio tried to create a convincing illusion of real life, the light in the painting does not align with the light from the windows in the Brancacci Chapel.

assessment_items.question_content:
**The Tribute Money is one of the earliest surviving examples of a painting that used linear perspective.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/848be18faf33e60c2dfa70ddf2932a3a.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**This is correct. The Tribute Money is one of the earliest surviving examples of a painting that used linear perspective.**

**[Watch this video to review](https://www.khanacademy.org/humanities/art-history/art-history-1400-1500-renaissance-in-italy-and-the-north/florence-1/v/masaccio--the-tribute-money--1427)**
